channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Crimson Desert Update Lets You Tame Special Mounts
item.content:encoded:
If you've been playing Pearl Abyss' tremendously popular Crimson Desert, you may have been wanting to change up your mount for something more exotic. Why travel around on a normal mount when far weirder options exist?

As part of Crimson Desert update 1.06 the following animals can be registered as 'special mounts':

  • Bears,
  • Boars,
  • Wolves,
  • Deer,
  • Mountain Goats,
  • Kuku Birds,
  • Iguanas,
  • Raptors,
  • Camels,
  • Lions, 
  • Tigers

Some of the animals will have unique taming methods, while those labeled ferocious will be tamable by subduing and then feeding them, gaining their trust. Feeding animals that aren't tamable won't impact their trust level. Saddles for special mounts can be found at different saddleries across the world, with 8 different saddlers having access to one or two special saddles each. Players will be able to equip both a special mount and a normal mount.

Beyond the special mounts and saddles, Crimson Desert update 1.06 also adds a new extraction feature. This feature can be used at various smithies and returns some of the material that was used to refine equipment, either for a single refinement level or all of them since acquired. Returns are based on material type - special materials like Artifacts and Aeserion's scale are recovered in full, while common materials like copper and iron return about 70% of the amount used.

Other changes include unarmed combat skills ('Fists') for Oongka, while Damiane will get some unarmed skills in the next update. Additionally, swords now have sheaths, which you can toggle an option on whether it is displayed or not. For those who want their canine friends to get in on the action, the new Sigil of Valor item, available from the Secret Shop NPC in Poroin, will have your pet dog attacks enemies when it is equipped. 

Beyond all of that there is a new claw machine at the Laughing Marionette with a number of items you can obtain from it. A number of smaller changes are also included in the update which you can read here.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don

More Info About This Game

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Retro-Inspired TTRPG Break!! Returns to Kickstarter With New Starter Supplement
item.content:encoded:
The award-winning TTRPG, Break!!, has returned to Kickstarter. Developer Grey Wizard's new crowdfunding campaign includes a new printing of the retro-inspired tabletop experience alongside a new starter set to bring new players into the game.

The Break!! and Start TTRPG Kickstarter Campaign

According to its campaign page, Break!! is a TTRPG inspired by classic JRPGs (Chrono Trigger, Secret of Mana, etc.) and fantasy anime like Nausicaa, Berserk, and Delicious in Dungeon.

The core rulebook is over 470 pages and contains a richly detailed, post-apocalyptic setting. The game is set in Outer World, a fantasy land of derelict mega structures, shambling war machines, and nature warped by magical pollution. It's also a world where half the world is bathed in light while the other is cast in shadow.

Adventurers in this land will explore the blighted landscape, interact with Outer World's inhabitants, fight or negotiate with dangerous monsters and enemies, craft wondrous items and social bonds with their teammates, and attempt to rise through the adventuring ranks to become legends.

The initial Kickstarter campaign for Break!! was a standout success. It garnered over 10,000 backers and raised over $580k during its run. The game also won the 2024 Gold Ennie award for Best Family Game thanks to its accessible system and rich original setting. If you missed that initial campaign, this new Kickstarter campaign is a way to have this game on your shelf at a crowdfunding discount.

For those who already own the Core Rulebook, this new Kickstarter campaign includes a new supplement. The Start Companion Book is a 200 page supplement designed to bring new players into playing the game as quickly as possible. The book includes a "tutorial adventure" to help players get used to the core rules, a full prewritten adventure, and a sandbox region for players to explore.

The pledge tiers for the Break!! Kickstarter campaign are as follows:

  • Start for $21: A PDF of the Start Companion Book
  • Start Physical for $48: A PDF and physical hardcover copy of the Start Companion Book
  • Core Rules + Start (PDF) for $48: PDFs of the Core Rulebook and Start Companion Book
  • Start + GM Screen for $68: PDF and physical hardcover copies of the Start Companion Book and a GM screen
  • Core Rules (Special) for $98: PDF and physical hardcover copies of the Special Edition Core Rulebook
  • Core Rules + Start for $109: PDF and physical copies of the Core Rulebook and Start Companion Book
  • Core Rules (Special) + Start for $136: PDF and physical copies of the Start Companion Book and the Special Edition Core Rulebook
  • Core Rules (Special) + Start + GM Screen for $164: PDF and physical copies of the Start Campanion Book, Special Edition Core Rulebook, and a GM Screen
  • Core Rules + Special + Start + GM Screen for $232: PDF and physical copies of the Start Companion Book, the Core Rulebook, the Special Edition Core Rulebook, and a GM Screen

At the time of writing, the Break!! Kickstarter campaign has raised $141,056, more than five times its initial funding goal of $$27,179. The campaign ends on May 28.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/retro-inspired-ttrpg-break-returns-to-kickstarter-with-new-starter-supplement
